input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to build a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server using stdio transport. They need:

1. A tool that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI)
2. A prompt template for summarization
3. Logging that doesn't interfere with stdio JSON-RPC protocol

Let me create a complete working project for them. I'll need to:

1. Create a C# project with the MCP SDK NuGet package
2. Set up the MCP server with stdio transport
3. Add a tool that uses HttpClient via DI
4. Add a prompt template for summarization
5. Configure logging to use stderr (not stdout) so it doesn't interfere with JSON-RPC on stdio


6. Start building out the project structure and implementation.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-c9d79cfbb1d343e88499ab94b8a344c4 && dotnet new console -n McpStdioServer --framework net10.0 -o McpStdioServer --no-restore 2>&1

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="10.0.7" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Http" Version="10.0.7" />
    <PackageReference Include="ModelContextProtocol" Version="1.3.0" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
